payload.text:
帮我创建一个合作&开发者中心模型，功能有如下这些：

	开发者注册
		注册流程指南
		注册申请
	开发者管理
		开发者授权
			资料审核
			账号授权
				软件授权
				开发资料授权
				功能授权
				API授权
			生成APPCODE+appkey
		开发者维护
			注销
			权限变更
			续期
	开发资料管理
	开发者APPCODE认证
		开发者资料获取
		插件发布
		插件接口对接
		模型配置
		输入APPCODE才能进入相应页面

**Defining the Application Scope**

I'm currently focused on defining the initial scope for the "Partner & Developer Center" web application. Right now, I'm diving into the core features: developer registration and management. Specifically, I'm outlining the developer management features, with an emphasis on account authorization details, including API and function controls. The user will be able to review profiles and make account authorization decisions.


**Refining Key Application Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the specific details of authentication with APPCODE and AppKey. The developer maintenance features, including deregistration, permission adjustments, and renewal, are also under consideration. Documentation management will be a pivotal part of this, with a focus on APPCODE authentication for data access, plugin publishing, model configuration and gated pages.

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { ShieldCheck, Key, Trash2, RefreshCw, Clock, MoreHorizontal, ChevronRight, Filter, CheckCircle2, XCircle, Code2, Lock, Copy, ExternalLink } from 'lucide-react'; import { RegistrationStatus, Developer } from '../types'; const MOCK_DEVELOPERS: Developer[] = [ { id: '1', name: '李小龙', email: 'bruce@lee.com', company: '功夫科技', status: RegistrationStatus.APPROVED, appCode: 'DC-8848-X', appKey: 'ak_prod_a1b2c3d4e5f6', permissions: { software: true, devDocs: true, functions: false, api: true }, expiryDate: '2025-12-31' }, { id: '2', name: '张建国', email: 'jianguo@corp.com', company: '建国集团', status: RegistrationStatus.PENDING, permissions: { software: false, devDocs: true, functions: false, api: false } }, { id: '3', name: '王小红', email: 'xiaohong@startup.io', company: '红红火火工作室', status: RegistrationStatus.APPROVED, appCode: 'DC-1024-Y', appKey: 'ak_test_z9y8x7w6v5u4', permissions: { software: true, devDocs: true, functions: true, api: true }, expiryDate: '2024-06-15' } ]; const Management: React.FC = () => { const [devs, setDevs] = useState(MOCK_DEVELOPERS); const [selectedDev, setSelectedDev] = useState(null); const [isAuthModalOpen, setIsAuthModalOpen] = useState(false); const [isKeyModalOpen, setIsKeyModalOpen] = useState(false); const handleOpenAuth = (dev: Developer) => { setSelectedDev(dev); setIsAuthModalOpen(true); }; const handleAuthorize = () => { if (!selectedDev) return; // Simulate generation of keys const updated = { ...selectedDev, status: RegistrationStatus.APPROVED, appCode: `DC-${Math.floor(1000 + Math.random() * 9000)}-Z`, appKey: `ak_prod_${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 12)}`, expiryDate: new Date(Date.now() + 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString().split('T')[0] }; setDevs(prev => prev.map(d => d.id === updated.id ? updated : d)); setSelectedDev(updated); setIsAuthModalOpen(false); setIsKeyModalOpen(true); }; const getStatusBadge = (status: RegistrationStatus) => { const styles = { [RegistrationStatus.APPROVED]: 'bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-700', [RegistrationStatus.PENDING]: 'bg-amber-100 text-amber-700', [RegistrationStatus.REJECTED]: 'bg-red-100 text-red-700', [RegistrationStatus.REVIEWING]: 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-700', }; const labels = { [RegistrationStatus.APPROVED]: '已授权', [RegistrationStatus.PENDING]: '待审核', [RegistrationStatus.REJECTED]: '已驳回', [RegistrationStatus.REVIEWING]: '复核中', }; return {labels[status]}; }; return (

开发者管理

集中管理开发者生命周期、权限变更与密钥认证。

{devs.map((dev) => ( ))}
开发者信息 状态 授权类型 APPCODE / 过期时间 操作
{dev.name[0]}

{dev.name}

{dev.company} · {dev.email}

 {getStatusBadge(dev.status)}
{dev.permissions.api && } {dev.permissions.software && } {dev.permissions.devDocs && }
{dev.appCode ? (
{dev.appCode}

{dev.expiryDate}

) : ( -- )}
{dev.status === RegistrationStatus.PENDING ? ( ) : ( <> )}
{/* Authorization Modal */} {isAuthModalOpen && selectedDev && (

资料审核与授权

申请人详情

姓名

企业

配置授权权限

{[ { id: 'software', label: '软件授权 (Software)', desc: '允许运行指定的专有软件环境' }, { id: 'devDocs', label: '开发资料授权 (Dev Docs)', desc: '获取完整 API 文档与示例代码' }, { id: 'api', label: 'API 接口授权 (API Access)', desc: '生产环境 API 调用权限' }, { id: 'functions', label: '功能授权 (Features)', desc: '高级功能模块与实验性插件' } ].map(p => ( ))}
)} {/* Key Result Modal */} {isKeyModalOpen && selectedDev && (

请妥善保存以下信息，切勿向第三方透露。

)}
); }; export default Management;

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { useParams, useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Puzzle, Settings2, Terminal, CloudUpload, MessageSquareCode, Database, ArrowLeft, CheckCircle2, AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react'; import { geminiService } from '../services/geminiService'; const DeveloperConsole: React.FC = () => { const { appCode } = useParams(); const navigate = useNavigate(); const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState('plugins'); const [chatQuery, setChatQuery] = useState(''); const [chatHistory, setChatHistory] = useState<{role: string, content: string}[]>([]); const [isAiLoading, setIsAiLoading] = useState(false); const handleAiAsk = async () => { if (!chatQuery.trim()) return; const userMsg = chatQuery; setChatQuery(''); setChatHistory(prev => [...prev, { role: 'user', content: userMsg }]); setIsAiLoading(true); try { const result = await geminiService.getDevAssistantResponse(userMsg); setChatHistory(prev => [...prev, { role: 'ai', content: result }]); } catch (e) { setChatHistory(prev => [...prev, { role: 'ai', content: '抱歉，AI 助手暂时无法响应。' }]); } finally { setIsAiLoading(false); } }; const tabs = [ { id: 'plugins', label: '插件发布', icon: Puzzle }, { id: 'api', label: '接口对接', icon: Terminal }, { id: 'model', label: '模型配置', icon: Database }, { id: 'settings', label: '授权设置', icon: Settings2 }, ]; return (

开发者空间 - {appCode}

认证成功 · 欢迎访问私有资源中心

{tabs.map(tab => ( ))}
{activeTab === 'plugins' && (

将您的能力封装为插件提供给平台用户。

{[ { name: '智能客服网关', version: 'v1.2.0', status: 'ONLINE', downloads: '1.2k' }, { name: '支付安全过滤', version: 'v0.9.5', status: 'BETA', downloads: '450' }, ].map((p, idx) => (
{p.status}

{p.name}

版本 {p.version} {p.downloads} 次调用
))}
发布更多插件
)} {activeTab === 'api' && (

接口对接控制台

// 初始化 SDK 示例

const client = new DevHubClient({`{`}

appCode: '{appCode}',

appKey: 'YOUR_SECRET_KEY'

{`}`});

认证接口 (Auth API)
200 OK

)} {activeTab === 'model' && (

)}

{chatHistory.map((msg, i) => (
{msg.content}
))} {isAiLoading && (
)}
setChatQuery(e.target.value)} onKeyDown={e => e.key === 'Enter' && handleAiAsk()} placeholder="询问开发问题..." className="bg-transparent border-none outline-none flex-1 text-sm py-2" />
); }; export default DeveloperConsole;

payload.text:
**Addressing the Import Issue**

I've identified the missing import in `pages/Management.tsx` for `FileText`. My focus is on resolving this import error specifically to ensure the component renders correctly. This is a straightforward fix, requiring a simple import statement to be added.


**Verifying the Import**

I've confirmed `FileText` is used in `pages/Management.tsx` within the component I'm trying to fix, at line 108. I also reviewed the error message, clarifying the line numbers don't perfectly align with the current snippet. The missing import is clearly the root cause.


**Confirming the Missing Import**

I've re-examined the code and confirmed that line 108 of the provided snippet in `pages/Management.tsx` does, in fact, use `FileText`. My focus has shifted to explicitly adding `FileText` to the `lucide-react` import statement within the file. I am also going to review the `@google/genai` instructions provided. I'm focusing on ensuring I correctly interpret and address this potential new aspect of the instructions.


**Evaluating the Data Structures**

I'm now diving into the structure of the data and the API interaction. I am taking a critical look at the `GenerateContentResponse` object and its text property, particularly in the context of the example provided. I think the key focus for me is to ensure that the approach of using `response.text` is the correct method, as the guidelines suggested. I'm also examining the implication of initializing GoogleGenAI at the top level and how it interacts with the response.


**Analyzing API Key Initialization**

I'm now focusing on API key handling, as it appears I initialized the GoogleGenAI instance at the top level. The guidelines suggest creating a new instance before each API call, though this might be specific to video generation. I'm ensuring I'm following the instructions to use `process.env.API_KEY` when initializing the client. I'm also confirming the prompt is correctly passed as a string for the intended text tasks, not video ones.


**Addressing Potential Error Handling**

I'm now assessing potential error handling issues within the `geminiService.ts` file. I've noted that using `JSON.parse(response.text)` could throw an error if `response.text` is undefined. This could occur in both the `reviewApplication` and `getDevAssistantResponse` methods. The undefined scenario is also possible when initializing the API key. My focus is now on confirming that `process.env.API_KEY` is always defined.


**Pinpointing the Import Location**

I've re-examined the code, verifying the use of `FileText` on line 108 of `pages/Management.tsx`. My focus is on the `lucide-react` import statement, confirming it needs `FileText` to resolve the import error. I've re-examined the file and have determined that the reference is on line 108, as previously stated.


**Confirming FileText Usage**

I've re-examined the line numbering, and I am certain `FileText` is used and the import is indeed the problem. However, I have verified that line 144 is *not* where `FileText` is used. I am going to focus back on the import statement for `lucide-react`. The problem is definitely with the import statement in `pages/Management.tsx`


**Refining the API Initialization**

I've re-examined the API key initialization, and I am changing it to `process.env.API_KEY` in `geminiService.ts`. I also confirmed, in `pages/Management.tsx`, that line 108 utilizes `FileText`, which means the import error remains the focus. I'm verifying the current imports to make sure `FileText` is included. I need to make sure I'm following the instructions closely and not assuming things about the key.


**Checking for other Imports**

I'm now checking for other potentially missing imports, and focusing on verifying that `FileText` is imported in `App.tsx` and `pages/Documentation.tsx`, while also being used in `pages/Management.tsx`. I am also verifying that the `process.env.API_KEY` usage adheres to the guidelines, and I am reviewing the `reviewApplication` method in `geminiService.ts`. I'm confirming `ai.models.generateContent` is being used correctly with the model name and prompt.


**Refining the Key Assignment**

I've now updated the API key assignment in `geminiService.ts` to explicitly use `process.env.API_KEY`, removing the potential for an empty string. Also, I've re-examined the code in `pages/Management.tsx` and have verified that `FileText` is used and needs to be imported. I'm focusing on ensuring it's added to the existing `lucide-react` import statement, per the instructions.


**Verifying Missing Import**

I'm now certain `FileText` is the key import needed in `pages/Management.tsx` to fix the error; it's absent from the `lucide-react` import statement. I've re-examined the key assignment in `geminiService.ts` and realized I should not use `|| ''`, as it might violate the guideline. I am now focused on adding `FileText` to the import and confirming the application's external key management.


**Evaluating Potential Errors**

I'm now focusing on a potential error related to `JSON.parse(response.text)` in `geminiService.ts`. I noticed this could fail if `response.text` is undefined. The code uses `response.text` as recommended. I need to make sure I add a check to handle the potential undefined case, and I'm ensuring all the prompts are properly passed. I am also checking that there aren't any other problems.


**Reassessing the Line Number**

I'm revisiting the line numbers to be sure about the `FileText` import issue. Initially, I focused on line 108, but the error report mentioned line 144. I've re-examined `pages/Management.tsx` and now I'm checking if `FileText` is used at line 144. I'm focusing on the component that has an `h3` tag at line 144 within the Authorization Modal content, and I'm ensuring I resolve it to the `lucide-react` import statement.


**Implementing the Import Fix**

I'm now going to focus on fixing the import issue. I've re-examined the code in `pages/Management.tsx` and have confirmed that the missing import of `FileText` is causing the problem. I've also found the issue in `services/geminiService.ts`, which I'm changing to adhere to the explicit API key initialization guideline. I am fixing it to be `const ai = new GoogleGenAI({apiKey: process.env.API_KEY});`. I will also correct the use of `response.text`.


**Verifying Text Response**

I'm now focusing on the type of `response.text`, ensuring it aligns with `string | undefined`. The current implementation technically works if it's a string, as anticipated, based on the provided guide and initial assumption that `response.text` is a string. However, I want to clarify whether I need to check for `undefined`. I've also reviewed the `gemini-3-flash-preview` and `gemini-3-pro-preview` models, and I am ensuring that the correct model is being used.


**Finalizing the Import**

I'm now certain `FileText` is the key fix needed for `Management.tsx`. I will add it to the `lucide-react` import statement. I'm also preparing to directly use `process.env.API_KEY` for API key initialization. My focus now is ensuring the application handles potential `undefined` states for `response.text`. I've prepared the changes to be implemented.


**Refining the Error Handling**

I'm now focusing on error handling within `geminiService.ts` to address potential `undefined` responses. My approach involves checking if `response.text` is undefined before attempting to parse it with `JSON.parse`. Per guidelines, I am using the `text` property, not a method. Also, the guidelines state I can assume the API key is accessible in the execution context, so the code is ready to handle that appropriately.

I'm now going to focus on adding an `undefined` check for `response.text` in `geminiService.ts`. I need to ensure it's not undefined before I attempt to parse it, preventing potential errors. I've also double-checked the API key initialization using `process.env.API_KEY`, which is now set and ready to ensure the service runs.


**Evaluating Model Selection**

I'm now re-evaluating the model choice for both tasks in `geminiService.ts`. I am considering that "reviewing an application" could fall under "complex text tasks," requiring `gemini-3-pro-preview` instead of `gemini-3-flash-preview`. However, the prompt might be basic enough for `gemini-3-flash-preview`. I am going to see how I can verify model choice by testing the result.


**Revisiting Error Handling**

I'm now implementing the `undefined` check in `geminiService.ts` before parsing `response.text` and confirming the API key initialization with `process.env.API_KEY`. While I've implemented the `FileText` import, I still want to revisit the potential for an undefined response and ensure that's appropriately handled. The API key access is also ready.


**Re-evaluating the Import**

I'm revisiting the line numbers and ensuring I resolve the `FileText` import issue in `pages/Management.tsx`. It is located on line 108, after reviewing the code. I am focusing on ensuring that the import is placed within the `lucide-react` import statement. I am preparing the final fix to the import.



**Implementing the Import Fix**

I'm now going to add `FileText` to the `lucide-react` import statement in `pages/Management.tsx`, per the error message. I'm also ensuring the API key is initialized correctly with `process.env.API_KEY` in `geminiService.ts`, as indicated in previous guidelines. I am also planning to implement the fix for `response.text`.


**Implementing the Fix**

I'm now adding the `FileText` import to `lucide-react` in `pages/Management.tsx` and adding a check for `undefined` on `response.text` in `geminiService.ts` before using `JSON.parse`. My focus is to resolve the import error and prevent parsing errors. I've re-confirmed that `process.env.API_KEY` is being correctly used, and I'm ready to test the changes.


**Refining the State Management**

I'm now integrating the provided developer data, which includes mocked developer objects with different statuses, permissions, and expiry dates, directly into the `Management` component. I am focusing on ensuring that the `MOCK_DEVELOPERS` data array is correctly used to initialize the `devs` state variable, verifying that the component renders this data accurately. I'm now integrating the developer data directly. entries.description: Add missing FileText import from lucide-react which was causing a compilation error.

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { ShieldCheck, Key, Trash2, RefreshCw, Clock, MoreHorizontal, ChevronRight, Filter, CheckCircle2, XCircle, Code2, Lock, Copy, ExternalLink, FileText } from 'lucide-react'; import { RegistrationStatus, Developer } from '../types'; const MOCK_DEVELOPERS: Developer[] = [ { id: '1', name: '李小龙', email: 'bruce@lee.com', company: '功夫科技', status: RegistrationStatus.APPROVED, appCode: 'DC-8848-X', appKey: 'ak_prod_a1b2c3d4e5f6', permissions: { software: true, devDocs: true, functions: false, api: true }, expiryDate: '2025-12-31' }, { id: '2', name: '张建国', email: 'jianguo@corp.com', company: '建国集团', status: RegistrationStatus.PENDING, permissions: { software: false, devDocs: true, functions: false, api: false } }, { id: '3', name: '王小红', email: 'xiaohong@startup.io', company: '红红火火工作室', status: RegistrationStatus.APPROVED, appCode: 'DC-1024-Y', appKey: 'ak_test_z9y8x7w6v5u4', permissions: { software: true, devDocs: true, functions: true, api: true }, expiryDate: '2024-06-15' } ]; const Management: React.FC = () => { const [devs, setDevs] = useState(MOCK_DEVELOPERS); const [selectedDev, setSelectedDev] = useState(null); const [isAuthModalOpen, setIsAuthModalOpen] = useState(false); const [isKeyModalOpen, setIsKeyModalOpen] = useState(false); const handleOpenAuth = (dev: Developer) => { setSelectedDev(dev); setIsAuthModalOpen(true); }; const handleAuthorize = () => { if (!selectedDev) return; // Simulate generation of keys const updated = { ...selectedDev, status: RegistrationStatus.APPROVED, appCode: `DC-${Math.floor(1000 + Math.random() * 9000)}-Z`, appKey: `ak_prod_${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 12)}`, expiryDate: new Date(Date.now() + 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString().split('T')[0] }; setDevs(prev => prev.map(d => d.id === updated.id ? updated : d)); setSelectedDev(updated); setIsAuthModalOpen(false); setIsKeyModalOpen(true); }; const getStatusBadge = (status: RegistrationStatus) => { const styles = { [RegistrationStatus.APPROVED]: 'bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-700', [RegistrationStatus.PENDING]: 'bg-amber-100 text-amber-700', [RegistrationStatus.REJECTED]: 'bg-red-100 text-red-700', [RegistrationStatus.REVIEWING]: 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-700', }; const labels = { [RegistrationStatus.APPROVED]: '已授权', [RegistrationStatus.PENDING]: '待审核', [RegistrationStatus.REJECTED]: '已驳回', [RegistrationStatus.REVIEWING]: '复核中', }; return {labels[status]}; }; return (

entries.description: Align with Gemini API guidelines: use direct process.env.API_KEY, and use the .text property correctly.

payload.text: 这不是一个独立产品，不需要概览页，或者概览页就是介绍我们软件数据交换产品的能力来吸引开发者注册，实际不会有很多开发者

**Reframing the Application**

I'm currently focused on how to restructure the "DevHub" application. The core aim is to remove the independent "Overview/Dashboard" as a separate entity and redesign the landing page. It's becoming an introductory point for the "Software Data Exchange," emphasizing its capabilities. This transition aims to streamline the user experience, clarifying the focus.


**Shifting the Focus**

I'm now prioritizing the new "Intro" page as the `/` route, replacing the dashboard. The goal is a high-conversion landing page. The sidebar navigation will update from "Overview" to "About Platform." We're adapting `Dashboard.tsx` to showcase data exchange, security, and developer workflow benefits. We are aiming for a refined "Partner Portal" aesthetic while retaining existing functionality. We are aiming to attract more developers through this shift in strategy, and also change the look of the home page to be less like a dashboard.

entries.description: Update navigation structure to focus on platform introduction instead of a generic dashboard.
New landing page focusing on software data exchange capabilities and attracting partners.

import React from 'react'; import { ShieldCheck, Zap, Database, Share2, Layers, Cpu, ArrowRight, CheckCircle, Code2 } from 'lucide-react'; import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; const FeatureCard = ({ icon: Icon, title, desc }: any) => (

); const Landing: React.FC = () => { const navigate = useNavigate(); return (
{/* Hero Section */}
全新一代软件数据交换

连接孤岛数据，
赋能软件生态

我们提供安全、高效、标准化的数据交换协议，助力开发者轻松打通跨平台业务流。 通过 DevHub，合作伙伴可以快速获取授权，发布插件并实现深度业务集成。

{/* Capabilities Section */}

核心交换能力

不仅仅是简单的 API 调用，我们构建了一整套覆盖资料获取、插件发布及模型配置的深度合作框架。

{/* Details Section */}

从注册到上线，
只需简单三步

{[ { step: '01', title: '注册申请', desc: '填写开发者信息与合作意向，通过 AI 智能初审与人工复核。' }, { step: '02', title: '资料获取', desc: '根据授权等级，获取专属 APPCODE 与全套技术开发包。' }, { step: '03', title: '对接发布', desc: '进行插件接口调试或模型配置，一键发布至交换中心。' }, ].map((item, idx) => (
{item.step}

{item.title}

{item.desc}

))}

"高效对接，生态共赢"

“作为合作伙伴，我们通过 DevHub 快速集成了核心业务模块，大幅降低了跨系统数据传输的研发成本。”

王经理

首席技术合作伙伴

{/* Final CTA */}

准备好提升您的数据交换能力了吗？

我们对合作伙伴的筛选保持严谨态度，以确保生态的高质量与安全性。

); }; export default Landing;
